result:
I’d give every child—no matter where or to whom they’re born—reliable access to clean air, safe water, nutritious food, basic health care, and a quality education through secondary school.  

It sounds simple, but hitting those five targets would break the generational cycles of poverty, poor health, and disenfranchisement that drive most other global problems. Pull that thread and you untangle a lot of the rest—conflict, displacement, even climate vulnerability—because people with security and education tend to have fewer, healthier children, invent cleaner technologies, build stronger institutions, and steward their environments better. One change, compound interest for humanity.

prompt: If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
